2606.24346.title: PETRA: Transforming Web Text for Petroleum-Engineering Domain Adaptation
2606.24346.paper_summary: Petroleum-engineering search exposes a supervision gap for strong general retrievers: relevant evidence exists in public web text, but domain relevance labels are scarce. To address this gap, we propose PETRA, a large-scale Petroleum Engineering Text for Retrieval Adaptation dataset and pipeline that converts noisy public web data into a curated domain corpus and synthetic supervision for dense retrieval and reranking. PETRA contains 1.36M curated chunks, approximately 2B token equivalents, $\approx$859k, embedding training rows from $\approx$224k anchors, and roughly 400k teacher-scored reranker candidate rows. Its construction combines high-recall energy-domain curation, an energy-domain classifier with 98.4% test accuracy, chunk-grounded query generation, LLM-written hard negatives, and retrieval-mined candidate lists. PETRA improves first-stage in-domain Normalized Discounted Cumulative Gain (nDCG) from 0.703 to 0.763 through score fusion. Reranker adaptation improves the public Earth Science benchmark by 44% relative and a six-task reasoning-intensive panel by 23%. Failed training recipes show that high train-holdout accuracy on synthetic labels does not predict retrieval gains; retrieval-mined data helps only after being repackaged as teacher-scored candidate lists sampled from the inference-time candidate distribution.
2606.24346.paper_summary_zh: 石油工程的研究揭示了強大通用檢索器的監督缺口：相關證據存在於公共網頁文本中，但領域相關性標籤稀缺。為了解決這一缺口，我們提出了PETRA，一個大規模的石油工程檢索適應數據集和流程，將嘈雜的公共網絡數據轉換為經過策劃的領域語料庫和密集檢索及重排序的合成監督。PETRA包含1.36M個經過策劃的塊，約2B的標記等價物，$\approx$859k，來自$\approx$224k錨點的嵌入訓練行，以及大約400k經教師評分的重排序候選行。其構建結合了高召回率的能源領域策劃、一個測試準確率為98.4%的能源領域分類器、基於塊的查詢生成、LLM撰寫的困難負樣本和檢索挖掘的候選列表。PETRA通過分數融合將第一階段的領域內標準折扣累積增益(nDCG)從0.703提高到0.763。重排序適應使公共地球科學基準提高了44%的相對增長，六項推理密集型面板提高了23%。失敗的訓練配方顯示，合成標籤上的高訓練-保留準確率並不預測檢索增益；檢索挖掘的數據只有在重新打包為從推理時候候選分布中抽樣的教師評分候選列表後才有幫助。
2606.24346.author: Kirill Dubovikov et.al.
2606.24346.authors: Kirill Dubovikov, Omar El Mansouri, Hachem Madmoun, Yanda Li, Sandeep Kumar, Aya El Mir, Supriyo Ghosh, Writabrata Bhattacharya, Adrian Garcia-Garcia, Onkar Pandit, Sunil Kumar Sahu, Federico Castanedo, Larry Murray, Martin Takac, Salem Lahlou
